description_long: This object is a vintage box-style television with a woodgrain-patterned exterior casing and a prominent convex glass screen. The front panel features two large rotary dials for channel and volume control, as well as a speaker grille and a small brand label. The television's body is rectangular, with the screen and controls occupying the left and right sides of the front face, respectively. The back of the unit is black, angular, and protrudes outward, typical of cathode ray tube (CRT) technology. The sides and top are finished in a dark wood veneer, giving it a classic, retro appearance. The overall build is robust and utilitarian, with visible ventilation slots on the rear for heat dissipation.
description: A vintage box-style television with a woodgrain exterior, convex glass screen, and large rotary dials on the front panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, two large rotary dials, speaker grille, and brand label.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the woodgrain finish and the protruding dials on the front edge.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the black, angular CRT housing and ventilation slots.
description_view_3: Right side view with a clear look at the woodgrain finish and the depth of the CRT housing.
attribute_bezel_design: Medium-thick, silver and brown bezel with woodgrain finish and metallic trim, rectangular styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two large rotary dials and a button cluster on the front right panel, arranged vertically.
